The browser you’re using is unsupported, and the website may not work correctly for you. It’s recommended that you upgrade to a recent version of Firefox, Safari, Edge, or any Chrome-based browser. If that’s not possible, you can try enabling legacy browser mode.

This is a MusicBrainz development server. Return to musicbrainz.org.

פוזי

~ Release by אריק איינשטיין (see all versions of this release, 1 available)

  • Overview
  • Disc IDs (1)
  • Cover art (0)
  • Aliases
  • Tags
  • Details
  • Edit
  • Edit relationships
  • Release information
  • Tracklist
  • Recordings
  • Edit note
The data you’ve seeded contained the following errors:
Review the documentation on release editor seeding and make sure your data is formatted correctly.
Release information
A release title is required.
You must select an artist for the release (the background color of the artist field should be green). If you’ve entered an artist but the background remains uncolored, click the magnifying glass to match the name to a MusicBrainz artist. If the artist doesn’t exist yet, you can add it by selecting “Add a new artist” from the bottom of the search results drop-down.
Checking existing release groups…
Release group search error Retry
Existing release groups with similar names:
If you don’t select an existing release group, a new one will be added with the types selected below.
You must select an existing release group.
To edit types, please edit the release group.
Release event
--
The date you've entered is not valid.
The year should have four digits. If you want to enter a year earlier than 1000 CE, please pad with zeros, such as “0123”.
You cannot use the same country more than once.
This label/catalog number pair already exists.
Additional information
External links

Loading...

The title is the release’s name.

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

The artist field contains the artist or artists as shown on the release.

Please see the artist credits documentation and style guidelines for more information.

The release group groups related releases into a single logical entity.

Examples of releases that should be grouped together include original releases, reissues, different formats such as CD and digital media, and remasters.

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

The primary type describes how the release group is categorized.

Secondary types are additional attributes describing the release group. Leave this blank if none apply. You can select multiple types by using Ctrl + click (or Cmd + click on a Mac).

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

The status describes how “official” the release is.

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

The pseudo‐release status should be used for unofficial translations and transliterations of tracklists and release titles, and does not denote a separate real release. It should not be used to denote bootlegs, mixtape/street albums, demos, or digital albums. Be sure to link to the corresponding real release with the transl(iter)ation relationship.

The language attribute should be set to the language used for the release title and track titles. It should not be set to the language the lyrics are written in, nor to the language used for other extra information on the cover.

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

The script attribute should be set to the script used for the release title and track titles.

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

The date describes when this particular release was made available.

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

“1990-10-25” is the bogus date that Amazon gives to all releases for which they don’t know the actual date. Please use this date only if you’re certain this date is correct.

If you do not know the month or day of release, please leave them empty. January 1st is not often the actual release date. Please double‐check that you have entered the release date correctly.

The country describes where the release was made available.

If you aren’t sure where it was released, please leave this unset.

The catalog number is assigned by the label and can often be found on the spine or near the barcode on physical media.

If you can’t find a catalog number, please leave this blank. If you are certain the release does not have a catalog number assigned, enter “[none]”.

The catalog number you have entered looks like an Amazon ASIN.

If this is an Amazon ASIN, please do not enter it in the catalog number field, but add it as a URL relationship later.

The label is the imprint associated with the release. Please also see the guidelines about special purpose labels.

The barcode is the numeric identifier (such as UPC or EAN) associated with the release.

Do not mark digital media releases as having no barcode unless you are familiar with the platform. Most mainstream digital platforms use barcodes, even when they are not shown.

If you don’t know whether the release has a barcode or not, please leave this blank.

The packaging attribute describes the outermost packaging of the release.

For digital media releases, this should usually be “None”.

The annotation field functions like a miniature wiki. It’s fine to leave it empty.

The purpose of this field is to add information that usually doesn’t fit into the strict structural data schema of MusicBrainz (be it due to technical limitations that may be addressed later, or because the information in itself has to be free‐form text).

This annotation will be published under an open license (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0) and as such, it should not contain text that you don’t have the right to release under that license. While you can quote a source to support a point you’re making, you should never enter promotional text or other artist- or label‐owned text into the annotation.

The disambiguation field helps users distinguish between releases in the same release group. Please leave it empty if other properties like artist, format, label, or date distinguish this release.

This field is not a place to store general background information about the release. That kind of information should go in the annotation field.

External links are URLs associated with this particular release.

Mail‐order links shouldn’t be attached to digital media releases, and download and streaming links shouldn’t be attached to CD or vinyl releases.

Please see the style guidelines for more information.

To enter a new tracklist, use an existing medium or import a disc from a CD stub, select the appropriate tab.

  • Manual entry
  • Existing medium
  • CD stub import

Use the following fields to search for an existing medium.

Use the following fields to search for a CD stub.

Add a medium by clicking “Add medium” below, or tick the box confirming the tracklist is unknown.

You’ve seeded some disc IDs that aren’t matched to any medium. If this is wrong, use the “Attach disc ID” options below.

(help)

A format is required. If you don’t know it, tick the “I don’t know” checkbox next to the format dropdown.

This medium has a disc ID, pregap track, or data track, so you can only change the format to one that can have disc IDs.

Warning:

If you’re sure the entered medium title is correct, confirm it above. Otherwise, please fix the data.

Warning: This medium is set to a format that did not exist on the selected release date.
This should never be correct. Make sure that, say, your release set to CD isn’t meant to be a vinyl, or your vinyl isn’t actually a shellac disc. This problem already existed before you opened the release editor, so you don’t need to do anything about it. But if you have some time, consider trying to find out what the real format should be or if the release should be split!

This medium is set to a format that did not exist on the selected release date.

Warning: This medium format is set to “Digital Media”, but the packaging type for this release is not “None”.

This is almost always incorrect. Please make sure whether you actually wanted a different format (such as “USB Flash Drive” or “Download Card”) or packaging type.

If you’re sure the combination of “Digital Media” and the selected packaging is correct, confirm it above. Otherwise, please fix the data.

Now loading tracklist...

Warning: You’ve used the Various Artists special purpose artist for some tracks below. Various Artists should very rarely be used for tracks; please make sure the artists have been entered correctly.

If you’re sure the use of Various Artists is correct, confirm it above. Otherwise, please fix the data.

Warning: This medium seems to have featured artists in the track titles. These should usually be entered as part of the track artist.

If you’re sure the use of featured artists in track titles is correct, confirm it above. Otherwise, please fix the data (the “Guess feat. artists” button might help!).

Warning: This medium seems to have capitalized extra title information in track titles. Descriptive parts of extra title information like “remix” or “version” should be lowercased in most languages.

If you’re sure the extra title information is capitalized correctly, confirm it above. Otherwise, please fix the data (the “Guess case” button might help!).

Warning: This medium may have incorrectly capitalized English track titles. Short articles, conjunctions, and prepositions like “and”, “of”, “or”, “to”, and “the” should typically be lowercased.

Common exceptions are artist intent and Japanese releases.

If you’re sure the titles are capitalized correctly, confirm it above. Otherwise, please fix the data (the “Guess case” button might help!).

Tracks are required. If you don’t know them, but do know some info (such as the format or number of media), tick the “I don’t know the tracklist for this medium” checkbox above and enter the available info. If you have no information about this release’s media, remove all media.

You must enter a title for every track (if the track is untitled, enter “[untitled]”).

You must select an artist for every track (the background color of every artist field should be green). If you’ve entered an artist but the background remains uncolored, click the magnifying glass to match the name to a MusicBrainz artist. If the artist doesn’t exist yet, you can add it by selecting “Add a new artist” from the bottom of the search results drop-down.

None of the attached disc IDs can fit a pregap track of the given length.

# Title Artist Length
Data tracks
Attach disc ID: link

Some discs contain a hidden track in the pregap section that precedes track 1. Use this to add (or remove) the special pregap track section.

Keep in mind that unselecting this will remove the track if the medium has a disc ID! If you unselect it by mistake, please readd the pregap track before submitting.

Some discs contain one or more data tracks after all audio tracks. Use this to add (or remove) the special data tracks section. You should only add data tracks that contain audio or video (more info)

Keep in mind that unselecting this will remove the tracks if the medium has a disc ID! If you unselect it by mistake, please readd the data tracks before submitting.

Add track(s)
Guess case

(help)

Note: All tracks require an associated recording. Click “Edit” to select a recording for each track. Choose “Add a new recording” if an appropriate one doesn’t exist yet.

Keep in mind that recording durations are recalculated from tracks once the duration of a track changes. As such, if you correct the duration of a track whose recording is only used in this release, just reuse the old recording, don’t add a new one. If the recording is also used elsewhere and the durations are significantly different, consider adding a new recording instead.

The tracklist for this medium is unknown.
track recording
(add a new recording)
by by

Click Edit to edit the recording associations for this disc.
Now loading recording associations...

If the recording titles/artists listed above are incorrect, you can check the boxes below to replace them with the track titles/artists for this release.

Note: In classical music, recording artists should almost always be different from track artists, so don’t use the “copy artist credits” option while editing a classical release unless you’re very sure it’s a good idea!

Options

Each track in the MusicBrainz database has to be linked to a recording, choose the appropriate recording or look for it in the database.

If we do not have a recording for this track in the database yet, please select "Add a new recording" below.

Search:

Looking for suggested recordings...

Suggested recordings:
appears on:

You haven’t made any changes!

(help)
